title: Session Lock Events for Workstation Inactivity Policy

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AC-11 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AC-11 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.22: Session Lock Events for Workstation Inactivity Policy.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.22' running on WinEventLog:Security 4800/4801, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4800,4801) earliest=-7d
| eval evt=if(EventCode=4800,"lock","unlock")
| stats count by evt, ComputerName
| xyseries ComputerName evt count
| eval lock_ratio=round(lock/(lock+unlock+0.001),3)
| where lock_ratio<0.2
description: Monitors workstation lock (4800) and unlock (4801) ratio for AC-11 session lock enforcement.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes sec and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Validate ComputerName field extraction; (2) exclude kiosk OU via lookup; (3) sample manual validation on outliers; (4) integrate with MDM compliance scores if available. Indexes required: `index=sec`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`.
visualization: Bar chart (lock_ratio by OU via lookup), Table (lowest hosts)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Access events during onboarding, M&A integration, controlled IT access reviews, or planned role transitions.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Validate ComputerName field extraction; (2) exclude kiosk OU via lookup; (3) sample manual validation on outliers; (4) integrate with MDM compliance scores if available.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4800,4801) earliest=-7d
| eval evt=if(EventCode=4800,"lock","unlock")
| stats count by evt, ComputerName
| xyseries ComputerName evt count
| eval lock_ratio=round(lock/(lock+unlock+0.001),3)
| where lock_ratio<0.2
```

Understanding this SPL

**Session Lock Events for Workstation Inactivity Policy (AC-11)** — Monitors workstation lock (4800) and unlock (4801) ratio for AC-11 session lock enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sec; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=sec, sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **evt** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by evt, ComputerName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pivots fields for charting with `xyseries`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **lock_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where lock_ratio<0.2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Session Lock Events for Workstation Inactivity Policy (AC-11)** — Monitors workstation lock (4800) and unlock (4801) ratio for AC-11 session lock enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (lock_ratio by OU via lookup), Table (lowest hosts)
grandmaExplanation: We watch workstation lock (4800) and unlock (4801) ratio for AC-11 session lock enforcement. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
